note: THE SINGLE shared exclude set, read by BOTH controls (cleanup + backup) and applied IDENTICALLY to every tree walk AND to the `tar --exclude=` invocation. One source, applied everywhere, so the backup expected-file walk and the archive entry set agree (the invariant that lets verify pass on a real ~/.claude). Each entry is a bare directory name and is matched UNANCHORED, at any depth — mirroring GNU tar's default `--exclude` behaviour (a matching directory and its whole subtree are skipped). These trees are transient/regenerable or not IOPHON-managed, so they are neither backed up nor scanned for layout drift.

rationale..git: version-control internals (including nested repos under plugins/) — regenerable from the remote, not config to preserve, and noise for a layout scan.
rationale.node_modules: vendored dependencies — regenerable from a lockfile, never IOPHON config.
rationale..pnpm-store: the pnpm content-addressed package store — a regenerable package cache, not config.
rationale.plugins: Claude Code plugin cache (plugins/cache/**). Its bundled SKILL.md / *.md files are plugin payload, NOT misplaced IOPHON docs — scanning it produced 2,585 false-positive 'stray skill' findings. Not IOPHON-managed.
rationale.projects: per-project conversation JSONL histories (multi-GB). Not §2-managed config and far too large to fold into a config-tree backup.
rationale.cache: any obvious cache dir — short-lived, regenerable, not config.
